layer_stability_disposition.note: 13-CONTEXT hard-constraint headroom obligation for the low-Jaccard-instability band {9,13,14,31,35,36} plus the late-layer-shift band {45,46,47} (output/sieve/prune_set_for_phase13.json layer_stability_notes). PRUNE-06's uniform-per-layer keep-count K makes a per-layer budget impossible (transformers num_local_experts is a scalar), so the obligation is enforced via the protected set: every flagged layer's protected experts are already counted in protected_expert_mask.npy and gate through the same physical-feasibility floor (k >= max_protected_per_layer, the global max over ALL 48 layers, = 40, driven by layer 1, which is not itself a flagged layer). Headroom check K >= 2x max_flagged_layer_protected_count (flagged-only max = 36, so 2x36=72; using the more conservative global max=40 instead, 2x40=80): K=96 (25%) passes both (96>=72 and 96>=80); K=64 (50%) FAILS both (64<72 and 64<80); K=32 (75%) fails both the headroom check and the absolute physical-feasibility floor (32<40).
layer_stability_disposition.candidate_winner_check_reason: verdict is no_winner (see per_variant reasons below) -- no candidate winner exists to assert the K >= 2x headroom check against. Recorded for the record: had AIMER@25 (K=96) passed its accuracy bars, it would ALSO satisfy the flagged-layer headroom check (96 >= 2*36=72), so headroom would not have been the disqualifying factor for K=96 -- the gen/judge/parse bars were.
